title: Node Graph Evaluation Engine
description: Build the evaluation engine that takes a directed acyclic graph of MeshNode instances, topologically sorts them, and evaluates each node in order by passing outputs from upstream nodes to downstream inputs. This is the runtime that executes a 'recipe' of mesh operations. Must support: cycle detection (reject circular graphs), type checking on connections, topological sort, and efficient re-evaluation when parameters change. Should serialize to/from JSON so LLMs can generate and modify node graphs programmatically. The JSON format is the DSL for procedural modeling.
definition-of-done: 1. NodeGraph class holds nodes and connections, supports addNode/connect/disconnect. 2. Topological sort produces correct evaluation order. 3. Cycle detection throws a clear error. 4. Type mismatch on connect throws a clear error. 5. evaluate() walks the sorted graph, creates NodeContext per node, wires outputs to downstream inputs. 6. JSON serialization round-trips a graph with 3+ nodes correctly. 7. Re-evaluation after changing a parameter produces updated output without rebuilding the graph.
testing-plan: 1. Build a 3-node graph: MeshCube -> Transform -> output. Evaluate and verify transformed mesh. 2. Attempt to create a cycle, verify error. 3. Attempt to connect FLOAT output to MESH input, verify type error. 4. Serialize a graph to JSON, deserialize, re-evaluate, verify identical output. 5. Change a parameter (cube size), re-evaluate, verify output changed. 6. Test a diamond-shaped DAG (one node feeds two downstream, both feed a join) to verify correct evaluation order.
todos: Define NodeGraph class with node storage and connection list
Define Connection record: sourceNodeId, outputName, targetNodeId, inputName
Implement addNode(String id, MeshNode node) and connect(Connection)
Implement topological sort using Kahn's algorithm
Implement evaluate() that walks sorted order, creates NodeContext, wires data
Implement JSON serialization: nodes (type + parameters) and connections
Implement JSON deserialization using MeshNodeRegistry to instantiate nodes by id
Add parameter change detection for incremental re-evaluation
unknowns: Whether to use pull-based (output node requests inputs recursively) or push-based (evaluate all nodes in topo order) execution -- push is simpler, pull enables lazy eval
Whether the JSON DSL should embed parameter values inline or reference them by name for LLM templating
